title: Creating Our Own Biographies and Autobiographies

questions.question: What does "interview" mean?
options: To ask questions of a person to get information from them.
To revise and rewrite a draft, making improvements.
The way in which something is built, arranged, or organized.
A first version of a piece of writing, not yet final.
questions.correct_answer: To ask questions of a person to get information from them.
questions.explanation: "interview" means: To ask questions of a person to get information from them.

questions.question: What does "structure" mean?
options: The way in which something is built, arranged, or organized.
A first version of a piece of writing, not yet final.
To ask questions of a person to get information from them.
To revise and rewrite a draft, making improvements.
questions.correct_answer: The way in which something is built, arranged, or organized.
questions.explanation: "structure" means: The way in which something is built, arranged, or organized.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Practice interviewing skills to ______ biographical information.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Practice interviewing skills to gather biographical information."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Learn to organize information logically, starting with key details.
Understand and apply the difference between first-person (autobiography) and third-person (biography) narration.
Draft and redraft a biography, focusing on standard English, correct spelling, and punctuation (semi-colons, colons, direct speech).
Practice interviewing skills to gather biographical information.
questions.correct_answer: Learn to organize information logically, starting with key details.
questions.explanation: Correct! "Learn to organize information logically, starting with key details." is one of the key things to remember.

questions.question: What does "draft" mean?
options: To ask questions of a person to get information from them.
To revise and rewrite a draft, making improvements.
A first version of a piece of writing, not yet final.
The way in which something is built, arranged, or organized.
questions.correct_answer: A first version of a piece of writing, not yet final.
questions.explanation: "draft" means: A first version of a piece of writing, not yet final.
